# SpaceX IPO Roadshow & June 2026 Timeline: Research Report

## Executive Summary

SpaceX's IPO is actively underway and is on track to be the largest initial public offering in history. The company filed its S-1 prospectus with the SEC on **May 20, 2026**, and is targeting a **June 12, 2026 listing on the Nasdaq** under the ticker **SPCX**. The IPO targets a valuation of approximately **$1.75 trillion** with a planned capital raise of approximately **$75 billion**. The investor roadshow began in **early June 2026**, following an accelerated timeline reported by Reuters on May 15.

## Key Timeline Events

| Date | Event | Source |
|------|-------|--------|
| **April 1, 2026** | SpaceX files confidentially to go public (S-1 filing with SEC) | NYT, Investopedia, Teslarati |
| **April 7-14, 2026** | Reuters exclusive: SpaceX lays out IPO details, targets early June roadshow; record retail allocation planned | Reuters |
| **May 4, 2026** | Index funds adjust rules to accommodate the mega IPO | Crypto Briefing |
| **May 14, 2026** | CNBC reports IPO prospectus could land "as soon as next week" | CNBC |
| **May 15, 2026** | Reuters exclusive: SpaceX accelerates IPO timeline, targets **June 12 listing on Nasdaq** | Reuters |
| **May 17, 2026** | Reports of stock split; BlackRock may invest up to $10B | Barron's, MSN |
| **May 20, 2026** | **S-1 prospectus filed publicly**, revealing financials for the first time | CNN, Reuters, NYT, NPR, Axios, multiple outlets |
| **May 21, 2026** | Massive media coverage; retail investor access confirmed through major brokerage platforms | CNBC, CNN, Bloomberg, BBC, Motley Fool |
| **May 22, 2026** | Space ETFs boom in anticipation; analysts warn of market impact | Reuters, CNBC, Barron's |
| **May 24, 2026** | Investor frenzy; index inclusion rules evolving; debate over whether IPO signals market top | Seeking Alpha, Business Insider, Barron's |
| **June 11, 2026** | Deadline for retail investor participation (per Gotrade) | Gotrade |
| **June 12, 2026** | **Target listing date on Nasdaq** | Reuters, TradingKey, multiple sources |

## Financial Details from S-1 Filing (Filed May 20, 2026)

The S-1 prospectus revealed SpaceX's financials publicly for the first time:

- **2025 Revenue**: $18.7 billion
- **2025 Net Loss**: ~$5 billion
- **Q1 2026 Additional Losses**: $4.3 billion
- **Target IPO Valuation**: $1.25–$1.75 trillion (some sources cite up to $2 trillion)
- **Target Capital Raise**: ~$75 billion
- **Ticker Symbol**: SPCX
- **Exchange**: Nasdaq
- **Prospectus Length**: 270+ pages

### Business Segment Breakdown
- **Starlink** (satellite internet): The profitable division, "paying the bills" — generating the bulk of revenue
- **xAI Division** (merged with SpaceX): Lost $6.4 billion in 2025 on $3.2 billion in revenue; $12.7 billion in capital expenditures (3x the rocket division's capex)
- **Rocket Division**: Core launch services
- **Total Addressable Market claimed**: $28.5 trillion (per Fortune)

### Governance & Ownership
- **Elon Musk controls 85% of shareholder votes** via super-voting Class B shares
- Musk will serve as CEO, CTO, and Chairman simultaneously
- Musk is effectively "unfireable" — he'd have to vote to remove himself
- **Musk's compensation**: 1 billion restricted shares tied to achieving (1) $7.5 trillion market cap and (2) a permanent Mars colony with 1 million inhabitants

### Related-Party Spending
- $700 million spent on Tesla "Megapack" products (2024–2025)
- $131 million on Tesla Cybertrucks (est. 1,183–1,813 vehicles, ~6–9% of all Cybertruck sales)

## Roadshow Details

- **Early June roadshow** was initially targeted (Reuters, April 7–14, 2026)
- The timeline was **accelerated** per Reuters (May 15, 2026)
- **Record retail investor allocation** planned — a departure from typical IPOs
- Major brokerage platforms confirmed to offer direct retail access (CNBC, May 21)
- **BlackRock** reportedly may invest up to $10 billion

## Market Impact & Analyst Reactions

- **Jim Cramer** warned the SpaceX IPO could be "destructive" for the rest of the market (CNBC, May 15)
- Analysts debate whether mega-IPOs (SpaceX + OpenAI) "could signal market top" (CNBC, May 22)
- Space ETFs are booming in anticipation (Reuters, May 22)
- SpaceX is set to join major indices after listing; index rules are being adjusted (Business Insider, May 24)
- The IPO could squeeze European IPO market (CNBC, May 19)
- Barron's warns it could tank Tesla stock as Musk shifts focus (May 24)
- If valued at $1.75T, **Musk could become the world's first trillionaire** (BBC, ABC Australia)
- Prediction markets (Hyperliquid) priced SpaceX above $2 trillion (May 19)

## Concurrent Mega-IPO Season
SpaceX is one of several anticipated mega-IPOs in 2026:
- **OpenAI** — expected in H2 2026
- **Anthropic** — expected in H2 2026
- Analysts warn these three could "drain 2026 AI IPO demand" (TradingKey, April 8)

## Future Ambitions (per S-1)
- Deploy "orbital AI compute satellites" (data centers in space) "as early as 2028"
- Establish lunar economy
- Transport humans/cargo to Moon and Mars
- Develop "human augmentation systems"
- Company acknowledges these involve "unproven" or nonexistent technologies
